topic_name: Russia Ukraine War & Sudan Civil War
subject: current-affairs
description: Russia Ukraine War & Sudan Civil War MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: When did the war between Ukraine and Russia (Russian invasion of Ukraine) start?
mcqs.explanation: Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine on 24 February 2022.

mcqs.q: In the Russian Empire, Ukraine became known by the geographic term:
mcqs.explanation: In the Russian Empire, Ukraine was historically referred to by the geographic term 'Little Russia' (Malorossiya).

mcqs.q: Approximately how many tanks does Russia possess?
mcqs.explanation: Russia is widely estimated to have around 7,000 tanks in total.

mcqs.q: The first Russo-Ukrainian War began in:
mcqs.explanation: The first Russo-Ukrainian War began in February 2014 after the annexation of Crimea and the start of the Donbas War.

mcqs.q: Following Ukraine's Revolution of Dignity, Russia occupied and annexed _____ from Ukraine.
mcqs.explanation: Russia annexed Crimea after Ukraine's Revolution of Dignity (Maidan Revolution) in 2014.

mcqs.q: In 2014, Russia supported pro-Russian separatists fighting against the Ukrainian military in the:
mcqs.explanation: Russia backed pro-Russian separatists in the Donbas War (eastern Ukraine) starting in 2014.

mcqs.q: Since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion in February 2022, approximately how many Ukrainian soldiers have been killed?
mcqs.explanation: Reports estimate roughly 43,000 Ukrainian soldiers killed since the start of Russia's full-scale invasion.

mcqs.q: The first Minsk agreement between Russia and Ukraine was signed in:
mcqs.explanation: The first Minsk Protocol was signed in September 2014 between Russia, Ukraine and the OSCE.

mcqs.q: Russia and Ukraine signed the Minsk II agreement in _____ to resolve the Donbas conflict; ceasefires repeatedly failed.
mcqs.explanation: Minsk II was signed in February 2015 in Belarus.

mcqs.q: When did Putin announce a 'special military operation' to 'demilitarise and denazify' Ukraine?
mcqs.explanation: Putin announced the 'special military operation' on 24 February 2022 — the start of the full-scale invasion.

mcqs.q: The International Criminal Court (ICC) opened an investigation into war crimes and issued arrest warrants for _____ and several other Russian officials.
mcqs.explanation: The ICC issued an arrest warrant against Russian President Vladimir Putin in March 2023 for the unlawful deportation of Ukrainian children.

mcqs.q: Military aid for Ukraine is coordinated through the Ukraine Defense Contact Group, which has _____ countries — including all 32 NATO members.
mcqs.explanation: The Ukraine Defense Contact Group has 57 member countries, including all 32 NATO states.

mcqs.q: _____ was a series of protests that led to political upheaval in Ukraine from late November 2004 to January 2005.
mcqs.explanation: The Orange Revolution was the 2004–2005 Ukrainian protest movement against electoral fraud.

mcqs.q: Russia failed to take control of Kyiv and withdrew from the areas of:
mcqs.explanation: Russia withdrew from both northern and northeastern Ukraine after failing to take Kyiv in early 2022.

mcqs.q: The Russo-Ukrainian War is currently being fought primarily in:
mcqs.explanation: The active fighting now centres on eastern and southeastern Ukraine.

mcqs.q: Russia launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine on 24 February 2022, starting the biggest war in Europe since:
mcqs.explanation: The Russia–Ukraine war is the largest war in Europe since World War II.

mcqs.q: The first phase of the Russian invasion of Ukraine ended on:
mcqs.explanation: The first phase of the war is generally considered to have ended on 7 April 2022 with Russia's withdrawal from Kyiv and northern Ukraine.

mcqs.q: In late 2021, Russia massed troops near Ukraine's borders and demanded a ban on Ukraine joining the:
mcqs.explanation: Russia demanded a ban on Ukraine joining the NATO military alliance — a key precursor to the 2022 invasion.

mcqs.q: The UN General Assembly passed a resolution condemning the invasion and demanding a full _____.
mcqs.explanation: The UNGA Emergency Special Session resolution demanded a full Russian withdrawal from Ukraine.

mcqs.q: The International Court of Justice ordered Russia to halt military operations, and the Council of Europe expelled:
mcqs.explanation: The Council of Europe expelled Russia in March 2022 in response to the invasion.

mcqs.q: Many countries imposed sanctions on Russia and its ally _____ and provided humanitarian and military aid to Ukraine.
mcqs.explanation: Belarus is Russia's principal ally in the Ukraine war and was placed under similar sanctions.

mcqs.q: Ukraine's parliament overwhelmingly approved finalising an association agreement with the EU in _____.
mcqs.explanation: Ukraine's parliament approved finalising the EU Association Agreement in 2013 — the rejection of which by President Yanukovych triggered the Maidan protests.

mcqs.q: Russia annexed Crimea in _____ after a widely disputed referendum held under occupation.
mcqs.explanation: Russia formally annexed Crimea in March 2014 following the disputed referendum.

mcqs.q: The Normandy Format meeting on _____ was the first and only meeting between Presidents Zelenskyy and Putin.
mcqs.explanation: The Paris Normandy Format meeting on 9 December 2019 was the only direct meeting between Presidents Zelenskyy and Putin.

mcqs.q: Who published the essay 'On the Historical Unity of Russians and Ukrainians', calling Ukraine 'historically Russian lands'?
mcqs.explanation: Russian President Vladimir Putin published the controversial essay 'On the Historical Unity of Russians and Ukrainians' in July 2021.

mcqs.q: The 2022 invasion began at dawn on 24 February 2022 with fighting initially in:
mcqs.explanation: Initial heavy fighting on 24 February 2022 erupted in Luhansk Oblast in eastern Ukraine.

mcqs.q: Immediately after the invasion began, who declared martial law in Ukraine in his first video speech?
mcqs.explanation: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy declared martial law in Ukraine on 24 February 2022 via video address.

mcqs.q: On 26 February NATO met and pledged military aid for Ukraine. On 27 February which country called the invasion a 'historic watershed' (Zeitenwende)?
mcqs.explanation: German Chancellor Olaf Scholz called the invasion a 'Zeitenwende' (historic watershed) in his 27 February 2022 address.

mcqs.q: The civil war between two rival factions of Sudan's military government began on:
mcqs.explanation: The Sudan Civil War broke out on 15 April 2023.

mcqs.q: The Sudan conflict involves the Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) and the paramilitary:
mcqs.explanation: The paramilitary force is the Rapid Support Forces (RSF).

mcqs.q: The Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) is led by:
mcqs.explanation: SAF is led by General Abdel Fattah al-Burhan, who chairs Sudan's Transitional Sovereignty Council.

mcqs.q: The Rapid Support Forces (RSF) is commanded by:
mcqs.explanation: The RSF is commanded by General Mohamed Hamdan Dagalo, popularly known as Hemedti.

mcqs.q: Which areas of Sudan are the centre of fighting?
mcqs.explanation: Both Khartoum (the capital) and the Darfur region in the west are the major battlegrounds.

mcqs.q: The Hala'ib Triangle is a disputed territory between:
mcqs.explanation: The Hala'ib Triangle on the Red Sea coast is disputed between Sudan and Egypt.

mcqs.q: Sudan has been described as facing the world's worst humanitarian crisis; nearly _____ people are experiencing extreme hunger.
mcqs.explanation: Approximately 25 million Sudanese face extreme hunger — the world's worst current humanitarian crisis.

mcqs.q: Since gaining independence in 1956, Sudan has endured chronic instability marked by how many coup attempts?
mcqs.explanation: Sudan has experienced about 20 coup attempts since independence in 1956.

mcqs.q: Which triangle is disputed among Kenya, Ethiopia and Sudan?
mcqs.explanation: The Ilemi Triangle is a disputed border area among Kenya, Ethiopia and Sudan.

mcqs.q: Which country has been widely reported as supporting the Rapid Support Forces (RSF)?
mcqs.explanation: The UAE has been widely accused of supporting the RSF in Sudan.

mcqs.q: On _____, General al-Burhan confirmed that the SAF had withdrawn from El Fasher, confirming RSF control over the city.
mcqs.explanation: On 28 October 2025, General al-Burhan confirmed SAF's withdrawal from El Fasher, North Darfur.

mcqs.q: The RSF attacked Port Sudan for the first time using drones on:
mcqs.explanation: The RSF launched its first drone attack on Port Sudan on 5 May 2025.

mcqs.q: Which neighbouring countries have welcomed Sudanese refugees in large numbers?
mcqs.explanation: Chad and South Sudan have hosted the largest numbers of Sudanese refugees from the civil war.

mcqs.q: The Wadi Halfa Salient is disputed between:
mcqs.explanation: The Wadi Halfa Salient is a small disputed area between Egypt and Sudan along the River Nile.
